subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Big Freeze
description: A quick, practical guide to The Big Freeze.

hook.text: If dark energy continues to push, the universe will expand forever. Stars will die, and darkness will reign.

buildup.text: In trillions of years, all stars will burn out. Galaxies will drift apart until none are visible to each other.

discovery.text: Black holes will slowly evaporate via Hawking radiation. Even they won't last forever.

twist.text: In the far future, only cold particles will drift through empty space. Temperature approaches absolute zero.

climax.text: This heat death is the most likely fate of the universe. Not a bang, but an eternal, silent freeze.

punchline.text: Not a bang at the end. A whisper. Then eternal silence.

quiz.question: What is the Big Freeze scenario for the universe's fate?
options: The universe expands forever until all stars die and temperature approaches absolute zero
The universe collapses back into a singularity
The universe splits into multiple separate universes
